#### Installation
- [Keenetic / NetCraze](https://keen-pbr.fyi/docs/getting-started/installation/keenetic/): keen-pbr can be installed on Keenetic / NetCraze routers via Entware&rsquo;s opkg package manager. …
- [OpenWrt](https://keen-pbr.fyi/docs/getting-started/installation/openwrt/): keen-pbr can be installed on OpenWrt routers from the keen-pbr package repository.
Check which …
- [Debian](https://keen-pbr.fyi/docs/getting-started/installation/debian/): keen-pbr can be installed on Debian systems from the keen-pbr APT repository.
Add the repository …

### Configuration
- [Outbounds](https://keen-pbr.fyi/docs/configuration/outbounds/): Outbounds tell keen-pbr where matching traffic should go.
Every outbound tag must match …
- [Lists](https://keen-pbr.fyi/docs/configuration/lists/): Lists are groups of sites or IP ranges that you want keen-pbr to match.
The lists key is an object …
- [Route Rules](https://keen-pbr.fyi/docs/configuration/route-rules/): Route rules connect your lists to your outbounds.
Most users only need one simple rule for each …
- [DNS](https://keen-pbr.fyi/docs/configuration/dns/): Use DNS settings when you want domains in a list to be resolved through a specific DNS server, …
- [Advanced](https://keen-pbr.fyi/docs/configuration/advanced/): Most users can skip this page.
These settings are for advanced setups and service-level tuning: …
